steps.title: Identify the logical relationship between sentences
steps.content: The first sentence states what Serrano wanted to avoid: harsh synthetic dyes. The second sentence describes what she then did: experiment with natural dyes. The second action is a direct response to the desire expressed in the first sentence, so the transition must signal cause-and-effect or purposeful follow-through.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice by logical fit
steps.content:
• Choice A — To sum up,: signals a summary of preceding ideas, but the second sentence introduces a new action rather than restating what came before. ✗
  • Choice B — In actuality,: signals a correction or contradiction, but her action aligns with her stated goal rather than contradicting it. ✗
  • Choice C — Nevertheless,: signals contrast or concession, but experimenting with natural dyes is consistent with wanting to avoid harsh synthetics, not contrary to it. ✗
  • Choice D — With this in mind,: means "keeping that goal in mind," correctly linking her desire to avoid harsh dyes with her decision to experiment with natural methods. ✓
D is correct.
